key_insight: The paper introduces a geometry-aware MCTS that incrementally enforces global geometric constraints, turning hard combinatorial-geometry search into a guided exploration problem.
engineering_relevance: It provides a practical search framework for computational geometry and discrete optimization when exact solvers and neural policies fail under sparse rewards and large action spaces.
method: Incrementally updates the feasible action space to strictly maintain geometric validity.
Uses geometric symmetries for canonical pruning to reduce branching.
Uses symmetric batch transitions to speed up discovery of promising configurations.
evidence_signal: Experiments on six benchmark problems report new best-known results on five, including Max-N3IL configurations of about 1.8n points for 82≤n≤119 and Smallest Complete Set upper bounds around 0.95n.
watch_item: Performance depends on efficient symmetry handling and constraint updates, which may become implementation-heavy for more complex geometric rules or larger grids.
paper_title: Geometry-Aware MCTS for Extremal Problems in Combinatorial Geometry
